title: The First Draft Is Supposed To Be Messy
short_teaser: Great creations usually begin as imperfect attempts.
story: Many creators, writers, inventors, and entrepreneurs begin with rough versions. The first idea is not the final product. It is a starting point that gives something to improve.
impact: Waiting for perfection often prevents ideas from becoming real. Creating something imperfect creates the opportunity to make it better.
spark_application: What could you start today if it did not have to be perfect?
